name: Google Analytics
description: Connects to the Google Analytics 4 (GA4) Data API and Admin API to sync analytics report data, properties, and metadata into the Shielva knowledge base. Authenticates via OAuth 2.0 (Bearer token, analytics.readonly scope).

install_fields.label: OAuth Client ID
install_fields.help: Your Google OAuth 2.0 Client ID. Created in Google Cloud Console under APIs & Services → Credentials → OAuth 2.0 Client IDs.

install_fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
install_fields.help: Your Google OAuth 2.0 Client Secret. Keep this secure — it is never logged.

install_fields.label: Redirect URI (optional)
install_fields.help: The OAuth redirect URI registered in Google Cloud Console. Optional — leave blank to use the Shielva default.

config_keys.description: OAuth 2.0 Bearer access token obtained after user authorization
OAuth 2.0 refresh token for obtaining new access tokens without re-authorization
ISO timestamp when the access token expires
GA4 Property ID (numeric, e.g. 123456789) — required for report queries

scopes.description: Read-only access to GA4 data, properties, and reports

methods.name: Install / Validate Credentials
methods.description: Validate that client_id and client_secret are present. Returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED or OFFLINE with reason. The actual Bearer token is obtained via the authorize() OAuth flow.

methods.name: Generate Authorization URL
methods.description: Return the Google OAuth2 authorization URL for the analytics.readonly scope. The user visits this URL, grants consent, and Google redirects back with an authorization code to exchange for tokens.

methods.name: Health Check
methods.description: Call list_accounts() to verify the Bearer token is valid. Returns HEALTHY with account count, OFFLINE on auth failure, or DEGRADED on network error.

methods.name: Sync
methods.description: Run sessions + users report for last 30 days on the configured property_id using default dimensions (date, sessionSource, sessionMedium) and metrics (sessions, activeUsers, screenPageViews, bounceRate). Paginates all rows via offset/limit. Normalizes each row to a ConnectorDocument.

methods.name: List Accounts
methods.description: Return all GA accounts accessible by the authenticated user.

methods.name: List Properties
methods.description: Return all GA4 properties under a given account ID.

methods.name: Run Report
methods.description: Run a GA4 Data API report for a property with given dimensions, metrics, and date range. Returns raw runReport response dict.

methods.name: Get Property Metadata
methods.description: Return GA4 property metadata including all available dimensions and metrics with descriptions.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Google Analytics connector** integrates with the Google Analytics 4 (GA4) Data API and Admin API. It enables Shielva to sync analytics report data, GA4 properties, and metadata into the knowledge base.

This connector uses **OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow** with the `analytics.readonly` scope. The access token is stored in config and used as a Bearer token for all API calls.

**Key capabilities:**
- OAuth 2.0 authorization with offline access (refresh token)
- Sync sessions + users analytics report rows for the last 30 days
- List GA accounts and GA4 properties
- Run custom reports via `runReport` (dimensions, metrics, date ranges)
- Run real-time reports via `runRealtimeReport`
- Retrieve available dimensions/metrics via property metadata
- Stable document IDs via SHA-256 for deduplication
- Automatic retry with exponential backoff on transient failures
- Offset-based pagination for large datasets
